---
title: "Assignment 1: The cocktail bar (data transformation and manipulation)"
author: "Tamas Nagy"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Skills needed to solve this assignment

-   Using R and RStudio, reading data
-   Reporting using RMarkdown
-   Using Git and Github (for submitting the task)
-   Data manipulation (e.g. dplyr, tidyr)
-   String manipulation (e.g. stringr)

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(tidyverse)

library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
```

# Background

Your Cuban uncle sets up a cocktail bar in downtown Budapest. He has a secret list of cocktails that he would like to serve in the bar. He asks you to do create a few lists and tables to set up the shop. As your uncle is a secret hipster, he has a dataset on Github that you can use for the task!

![](https://media1.tenor.com/images/57a519cffd0ad4693be3b9c94b211540/tenor.gif?itemid=5195211){width="320"}

Note: There are several different ways to solve these tasks, feel free to come up with your own.

## 1. Read the data

Read the cocktail dataset from: <https://github.com/nthun/cocktail-balance> You can also find the codebook there.

```{r}
path_to_df = "cocktail_data.tsv"

df = read.csv(
  path_to_df,
  sep="\t",  # tab separator instead of default comma
  )

df = data.table(df)

# head(df)  # display of dataframes is generally ugly, so don't :)
```

## 2. Transform the data table and clean the ingredient variable!

The ingredients are currently in a single cell for each cocktail. It would be better to put them in separate rows. Also, the variable should be cleaned of all quantities (e.g. 1/2 oz, 2 dashes, etc.), and the indicator of alcohol content (e.g. 47.3% abv). You will need to use this cleaned table in later tasks.

```{r}
ingredients = df$ingredients

# we do text cleanup in "reverse" order - from complex regexes to simpler ones - to keep them consistent/readable after each operation
# https://stringr.tidyverse.org/reference/str_replace.html

# removing alcohol content
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, " \\(\\d+\\.\\d+% abv\\)", "")  # " (X.Y% abv)"
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, " \\(\\d+% abv\\)", "")  # " (X% abv)"

# removing ounces
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+\\.\\d+ oz ", "")  # "X.Y oz "
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+ \\d+/\\d+ oz ", "")  # "X Y/Z oz "
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+/\\d+ oz ", "")  # "X/Y oz "
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+ oz ", "")  # "X oz "

# removing dashes, drops, spoons
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+ dashes ", "")  # "X dashes "
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+ dash ", "")  # "X dash "
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+ drops ", "")  # "X drops "
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+ drop ", "")  # "X drop "
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "\\d+ bsp ", "")  # "X bsp "

# tidying on separators
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "<br/>", ",")
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "<br>", ",")
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "<b4/>", ",")

# some spotted errors
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "cr\u008fme", "créme")  # 20th Century Cocktail
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "sugard", "sugared")  # Blender Daiquiri
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, " orange", "orange")  # Blood and Sand
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "gin ,", "gin,")  # Carbonated Negroni
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, " or ", ",")  # Carbonated Negroni
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "simple,", "simple syrup,")  # Gin and Juice Centrifuge
ingredients = str_replace_all(ingredients, "Marschino,", "Maraschino")  # Last Word

ingredients = tolower(ingredients)

# new table with only names and corresponding ingredients
# https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/reference/separate_longer_delim.html
df2 = data.table(df$name, ingredients)
df2 = separate_longer_delim(df2, c("ingredients"), ",")

# renaming and dropping duplicates (e.g. egg white in Clover Club)
df2 = df2 %>%
  select(cocktail=V1, ingr=ingredients) %>%
  distinct()

head(df2, 10)
```

## 3. All ingredients in alphabetical order

Before opening the bar, you need to find a reliable supplier that has all the ingredients. You need to send a list of all possible ingredients you will need. They don't need the quantities (i.e. how many of these are needed), just the names of the ingredients.

```{r}
df2 %>%
  select(ingr) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  arrange(ingr)
```

## 4. Number of unique ingredients

How many different ingredients you will need?

```{r}
df2 %>%
  select(ingr) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  count()
```

## 5. What are the top 10 ingredients?

What are the 10 most frequently used ingredients? If there are ties, you can list more than 10.

```{r}
df2 %>%
  count(ingr, sort=TRUE) %>%
  mutate(r = min_rank(desc(n))) %>%
  filter(r <= 10) %>%
  select(ingr)
```

## 6. Which cocktail(s) has/have the most ingredients?

Count the number of ingredients and filter all the cocktails that has that many.

```{r}
df2 %>%
  count(cocktail, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(r = min_rank(desc(n))) %>%
  filter(r == 1) %>%
  select(cocktail)
```

## 7. How many ingredients appear in only one cocktail (rare ingredient)?

Count all cocktails with only one ingredient, and

```{r}
# and..? :D

rare_ingr_cocktails = df2 %>%
  count(ingr) %>%
  filter(n == 1) %>%
  inner_join(df2, by = "ingr") %>%
  select(cocktail) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  arrange(cocktail)

rare_ingr_cocktails
```

## 8. Which cocktail has an ingredient that is only used in one cocktail?

```{r}
# I don't understand this question... :D Isn't this the previous one framed in a convoluted manner?
```

## 9. What are the cocktails without rare ingredients?

```{r}
df2 %>%
  left_join(rare_ingr_cocktails %>% 
              mutate(x = 1), by = "cocktail"
            ) %>% 
  filter(is.na(x)) %>%
  select(cocktail) %>%
  distinct %>%
  arrange(cocktail)
```

## 10. Create a cheat sheet for the bartender!

Create a matrix that shows all cocktail names as rows and all ingredients as columns. When a cocktail requires an ingredient, there should be an "X" in the cell, otherwise, the cell should remain empty. Example:

```{r echo=FALSE}
tribble(~name, ~Lime, ~`White rum`, ~`Cane sugar`, ~`Coca-cola`, ~Mint, ~Soda,
        "Caipirissima", "X", "X", "X", "", "", "",
        "Cuba Libre", "X", "X", "", "X","","",
        "Mojito", "X", "X", "", "", "X", "X") %>% 
    relocate(name, sort(names(.)))
```

```{r}
df2 %>%
  mutate(x="X") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from=ingr, 
    values_from=x, 
    values_fill=""
  )
```


Congrats, the bar is now officially open!

![](https://i.pinimg.com/originals/4e/c1/0c/4ec10c9d32b2c7c28b4b638c7f809ec5.gif){width="320"}
